question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the mirror node with a high volume of publish events so that the default no-op onPublish() implementation in MirrorSubscriber silently drops responses, causing records to be missing from the subscriber pipeline without any error signal?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where the Flux<SubscribeResponse> returned by subscribe() completes prematurely due to an unhandled upstream error, causing all subsequent mirror node records to be silently missing from the exported stream?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user query the /api/v1/subscriber endpoint to enumerate all active Scenario subscriptions and then craft targeted traffic to exhaust a specific subscription's limit, causing that scenario to stop exporting records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Flux returned by subscribe() to emit duplicate SubscribeResponse records by exploiting the retry logic in GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe(), leading to incorrect record counts exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a PublishResponse with a null TransactionId so that RestSubscriber.toString(TransactionId) throws a NullPointerException, causing the onPublish() pipeline to silently drop that record and produce a missing export to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed gRPC responses to the mirror node endpoint so that GrpcClientSDK.subscribeToClient() emits an error that is swallowed by the doOnError(subscription::onError) handler, causing records to be missing from the exported stream without alerting operators?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authentication on GET /api/v1/subscriber to enumerate scenario names and IDs, then use that information to predict and interfere with topic subscriptions, causing incorrect records to be exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the CompositeSubscriber.subscribe() flatMap to silently skip a subscriber's Flux by injecting an empty Flux from one of the MirrorSubscriber implementations, resulting in missing records from that subscriber in the exported output?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the directBestEffort() sink in RestSubscription to cause tryEmitNext() to silently fail when the sink has no subscribers, resulting in published records being dropped and missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery() to set an incorrect startTime by manipulating the consensus timestamp of a prior message, causing the subscription to skip records and export an incomplete set to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a TopicMessage with a crafted consensusTimestamp that is far in the future, causing GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery() to set a startTime that skips all real records, resulting in missing exports to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause shouldSample() in RestSubscriber to always return false by manipulating the samplePercent configuration path, so that no PublishResponse events are ever forwarded to the sink, resulting in zero records exported via REST?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Retry.backoff() in GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe() with maxAttempts set to Long.MAX_VALUE to keep the subscriber in an infinite retry loop, preventing it from ever successfully exporting records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe() to silently drop records by triggering onErrorResume(e -> Mono.empty()) through repeated 5xx errors, so that transactions are never exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the take(properties.getLimit(), true) in RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe() by causing the limit to be reached prematurely through injecting spurious responses, causing the subscription to terminate early and miss subsequent records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the take(properties.getDuration()) in RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe() to expire before all records are exported by delaying responses, resulting in missing records in the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: onPublish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause CompositeSubscriber.onPublish() to silently skip a subscriber by injecting an exception in one subscriber's onPublish() that propagates and short-circuits the forEach loop, causing records to be missing from downstream subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the secureRandom.nextInt() client selection in GrpcClientSDK.subscribe() to cause load imbalance where one client is overwhelmed and drops messages, resulting in missing records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user send a gRPC INVALID_ARGUMENT status to the mirror node so that shouldRetry() returns false and the subscription terminates permanently, causing all future records to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscription.onNext() sequence number check, which only logs a warning without halting the stream, to inject out-of-order messages that are silently accepted and exported as correct records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause GrpcClientSDK.toResponse() to produce a SubscribeResponse with a null publishedTimestamp by injecting a TopicMessage with invalid contents, causing SubscribeMetrics.onNext() to skip latency recording and export incorrect metrics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the Utility.getTimestamp() call in GrpcClientSDK.toResponse() to return null by crafting a TopicMessage with malformed contents, resulting in a SubscribeResponse with a null publishedTimestamp that silently corrupts latency metrics exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::createSubscriptions).cache() pattern in GrpcSubscriber to cause a race condition where subscriptions are cached before all properties are fully initialized, resulting in missing or incorrect subscriptions being exported?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the RestApiClient.retrieve() to silently return an empty Mono by triggering an error that is absorbed by onErrorResume(Mono::error), causing the transaction lookup to fail and the record to be missing from the mirror node export?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StringUtils.split(timestamp, '.') parsing in RestSubscriber.toResponse() by injecting a consensusTimestamp with more than two dot-separated parts, causing Long.parseLong() to parse incorrect values and export wrong timestamps to the mirror node?
